name: Hot Phyllo Burritos
recipeIngredient: 1 can (16 oz) pinto beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the green tops from onions. Cook the tops in boiling water for 1 minute; drain.
2. Cool the onions quickly by plunging them into ice water; drain again.
3. Cut each onion piece lengthwise into 3 or 4 strips and set aside.
4. Chop the white portions of the onions to measure 1/2 cup.
5. Process the chopped white portion, beans, and the remaining ingredients in a food processor until smooth, stopping to scrape down sides as needed; set aside.
6. Place one phyllo sheet on a large cutting board and coat with vegetable cooking spray. Keep remaining sheets covered with a slightly damp towel.
7. Stack three more phyllo sheets over the first sheet, coating each with vegetable cooking spray. Cut the stack lengthwise, then cut each half crosswise into thirds.
8. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of bean mixture near one long edge of each stack. Roll up starting at the same edge. Pinch the rolls about 1 1/2 inches from each end and tie with green onion strips. Place on lightly greased baking sheets. Repeat this process three times with remaining phyllo stacks and bean mixture.
9. Bake at 400°F for 10 to 15 minutes, or until golden; cool on wire racks.
10. Serve with additional salsa.
Makes 2 dozen.
